public_goals: Make sure the school transition, therapy schedule, and weekday plan stay coherent.
Avoid a partial agreement that looks workable on paper but collapses once transport and reimbursement are considered.
Reach a realistic next step without pretending the linked decisions are simpler than they are.
private_concerns: Worries that separating the issues will produce a false sense of progress.
Feels pressure to agree quickly even though the interdependence is real.
Does not want to be blamed later for logistics or cost consequences that were never fully mapped.
red_lines: Will not accept a school or weekday change that ignores transport and therapy timing.
Will not agree to reimbursement expectations that remain ambiguous once the schedule changes.
emotional_triggers: pressure to solve one piece in isolation
hidden cost or transport consequences being waved away
quick agreement framing that ignores interdependence
disclosure_tendencies: adds detail as soon as downstream effects are taken seriously
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes more resistant if complexity is treated as stalling
becomes more open when the process names the linked decisions explicitly
interest_profile: Not being trapped by partial agreements
starting_positions: Primary position: the school, therapy, transport, and weekday questions are all connected and should not be split up casually.
Secondary position: a realistic next step may need a broader human review before any package is treated as settled.
likely_openings: If we solve one piece in isolation, we are just creating a different problem two days later.
The timing, transport, and cost pieces all move together here.
